title: 'I was broken after the NZ series': Samson wins Player of the Tournament in T20 WC
content: Sanju Samson's stellar performance earned him Player of the Tournament in the T20 World Cup 2026, driving India's victory. Despite starting as a reserve, Samson amassed 321 runs in five matches, including three crucial half-centuries. His record-breaking feats and inspiring journey, including guidance from Sachin Tendulkar, highlight a career-defining tournament.

title: Amazon sends letter to FCC saying: Reject application of Elon Musk's Spacex for Space data centers
content: Amazon's Project Kuiper has urged the FCC to reject SpaceX's proposal for a million-satellite constellation, citing a lack of essential technical and orbital parameters. Amazon argues the plan is speculative, lacks space safety details, and would take centuries to deploy, potentially hoarding orbital resources.

title: Mid East tensions: UAE on high alert in Abu Dhabi as defenses respond to missile threat
content: Abu Dhabi residents were placed on high alert as the UAE's defence forces responded to incoming aerial threats amid escalating regional tensions. Emergency notifications urged citizens to seek shelter as air defence systems engaged potential missile and drone threats, with loud explosions reported. The incident underscores the spillover effects of the wider Middle East conflict impacting Gulf states.

title: Groups representing 700,000 Amazon, Google, Microsoft workers urge firms to ‘do right’
content: Employees from major tech firms like Amazon, Google, and Microsoft are demanding their companies reject Pentagon contracts that could compromise AI safety guardrails.  The open letter, from 'No Tech For Apartheid,' urges leaders to refuse demands that would allow AI for mass surveillance and autonomous weaponry, citing concerns over complicity in violence and repression.

title: Salesforce CEO on layoffs at Jack Dorsey’s Block: Company has its own issues
content: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff dismissed Block's massive layoffs as company-specific issues, not an AI-driven jobs apocalypse. He argued AI boosts productivity without wholesale cuts, citing Salesforce's growth. Skeptics, however, suggest Block's cuts are due to pandemic-era bloat rather than AI advancements.

title: Why are Indian investors pouring billions into Dubai real estate?
content: Indian investors are significantly fuelling Dubai's booming real estate market in 2026, injecting billions annually. Attracted by tax-free returns, strong rental yields and investor-friendly policies, they are now the largest foreign buyer group. This trend highlights Dubai's appeal as a secure global wealth hub, with even middle-class Indians diversifying portfolios overseas.

title: Stranded amid Iran vs US-Israel war? Know your rights on salary, leave as per UAE law
content: Thousands of UAE residents stranded abroad due to travel disruptions amid Iran vs US-Israel war, face salary and job uncertainty. UAE labour law offers protections, with employers expected to assess situations reasonably. Communication and documentation are crucial for employees, who may be eligible for remote work or paid/unpaid leave. Termination is not automatic for justified absences.

title: How Ishan Kishan fought personal grief to rise on the biggest night of his career
content: Battling personal tragedy after losing close relatives in a car accident, Ishan Kishan produced a match-winning performance in the T20 World Cup final. Despite the grief back home, the wicketkeeper-batter rose on the biggest night of his career to help India lift the title. Ishan scored 54 runs and picked up three catches in the final against New Zealand.

title: Nuclear stockpile to Kharg Island: Why Trump wants to send special forces to Iran
content: The United States and Israel are reportedly discussing deploying special forces into Iran to secure its highly enriched uranium stockpile. President Trump has not ruled out sending ground troops under exceptional circumstances, emphasizing the need to prevent Iran from acquiring nuclear weapons. Discussions also include options like removing or diluting the uranium on-site.

title: How Middle East crisis is making life difficult in Bangladesh and Pakistan
content: Escalating West Asian conflict triggers economic fallout in South Asia, with Bangladesh implementing fuel rationing amid panic buying and Pakistan facing shortages and price hikes. Border trade disruptions in Balochistan exacerbate the crisis, impacting essential commodity supplies and livelihoods.

title: Beedi in the loo: Passenger on Delhi-Goa flight booked for smoking mid-air
content: A passenger was booked after allegedly smoking a beedi in a Delhi-Goa Akasa Air flight lavatory on Saturday. Authorities discovered the man, identified as Ashish from Delhi, with a lighter, posing a significant safety risk to the aircraft and its occupants. A case has been registered under relevant aviation safety laws, with further investigation ongoing.

title: Sanju Samson scripts history, breaks multiple records in T20 World Cup final
content: Sanju Samson's explosive 89 in the T20 World Cup final powered India to a historic feat, with the top three batters scoring 50+. Samson also set a new record for most sixes in a single tournament with 21. He joins an elite group with semi-final and final fifties, and matches consecutive 50+ scores, solidifying his status as a tournament standout.

title: Salim Merchant on AR Rahman’s ‘communal bias’ row
content: Music composer Salim Merchant has responded to A. R. Rahman's remarks about potential communal bias in Bollywood. Merchant stated he doesn't personally believe bias plays a role, citing Rahman's prominent role in the upcoming 'Ramayana' film as evidence of the industry valuing talent irrespective of religion.

title: ‘Trump has caught hold of Modi’s nose’: Kharge slams PM over US ties & Middle East crisis
content: Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge fiercely criticized Prime Minister Modi, calling him a "slave" to US President Trump. Kharge alleged Modi's foreign policy compromises India's interests, citing trade issues and the Middle East crisis. He questioned Modi's silence on perceived US injustices and accused the government of neglecting governance for election campaigning.

title: 'Why? Why? Why?' Mamata questions timing of President venue row before Bengal polls
content: Speaking from the central Kolkata sit-in platform, Banerjee responded to Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s criticism earlier in the day, in which he accused the TMC government of “insulting” a woman tribal leader and the President of the country. In reply, Banerjee displayed an enlarged photograph showing the Prime Minister seated beside veteran BJP leader LK Advani while the President stood nearby.

title: Jr NTR’s visit at KIMS Hospitals sparks chaos, fans gather in large numbers
content: Jr NTR's hospital inauguration in Bengaluru turned chaotic as massive fan turnout overwhelmed security, leading to an escalator malfunction and a mild lathi charge by police. Despite the disorder, the actor maintained composure, waving to fans before proceeding with the event. The incident sparked online debate about fan frenzy and event safety.

title: Record alert in final! India rewrite T20 World Cup history vs New Zealand
content: India shattered T20 World Cup final records, posting a colossal 255/5 against New Zealand. Sanju Samson led the charge with a blistering 89, the highest individual score in a final. An explosive batting display, including a 98-run opening stand, ensured a commanding total, underlining India's dominance in the shortest format.

title: Oli’s tabla gift, 'riyaaz' advice to Balen 4 yrs ago turns into Nepal poll’s sharpest irony
content: Former PM KP Sharma Oli, who once gifted Kathmandu Mayor Balendra Shah a tabla, congratulated him on his recent electoral victory. Their relationship, initially cordial, soured significantly, culminating in a bitter election contest where Shah defeated Oli by a wide margin, highlighting a stark political shift.

title: T20 World Cup Final: India attempt to defend crown, script record third T20 title in Ahmedabad
content: India stands on the brink of a historic third T20 World Cup title, facing New Zealand in Sunday's final at the Narendra Modi Stadium. Aiming to become the first team to defend their title, Suryakumar Yadav's men carry the weight of expectation, seeking redemption on a ground that has previously seen their World Cup final defeats.

title: ‘Apocalyptic’ scenes in Tehran: Massive inferno erupts after explosions – video
content: Israeli airstrikes targeted multiple fuel storage facilities in Tehran and Karaj, causing large fires and explosions. The Israeli military confirmed the strikes, stating the facilities supported Iran's armed forces. In retaliation, Iran's Revolutionary Guards launched a new wave of attacks against Israel, which also reportedly struck ballistic missile sites. At least four people were killed in the strikes.

title: Abhishek, Samson tear into NZ; break 17-year record in T20 World Cup final
content: India's opening duo, Abhishek Sharma and Sanju Samson, powered to a record 98-run partnership in the T20 World Cup final against New Zealand. Sharma's rapid fifty set the tone, continuing Samson's strong tournament form. This explosive start provided India with a significant advantage as they aim for a historic third title on home soil.

title: 'Will not sign other bills until Save America Act is passed': Donald Trump
content: President Trump declared he will not sign any further legislation until Congress passes the Save America Act, deeming it a top priority. He urged lawmakers to expedite its passage, even suggesting a filibuster.

title: Texas rolls SC for 1st women's SEC tourney title
content: Madison Booker scored 18 points as No. 4 Texas sprinted to a 14-0 lead against No. 3 South Carolina en route to the Longhorns' first women's SEC tournament title.

title: Spartans' Fears gets tech for another groin kick
content: Michigan State point guard Jeremy Fears Jr. received another technical foul for a kick toward a player's groin area.

title: Sources: Conner reworks deal to stay with Cards
content: The Cardinals and Pro Bowl running back James Conner have agreed to a revised contract to keep him in Arizona for 2026, according to NFL Network.

title: UCLA rolls to 2nd straight Big Ten tourney title
content: Gianna Kneepkens scored 19 points and Kiki Rice added 15 points and eight assists Sunday as No. 2 UCLA rolled past No. 9 Iowa 96-45 to win its second straight Big Ten tournament crown.

title: Queens earns NCAA berth in first year eligible
content: Chris Ashby made 10 3-pointers and scored 34 points to lead Queens University to an NCAA berth in its first year of eligibility with a 98-93 win over Central Arkansas in the Atlantic Sun tournament.

title: 76ers star Maxey sidelined with right finger sprain
content: Philadelphia 76ers All-Star guard Tyrese Maxey suffered a sprain of his right fifth finger and will be sidelined for the upcoming back-to-back while the team develops a treatment plan.

title: GA Tech fires Stoudamire after last-place finish
content: Georgia Tech has fired men's basketball coach Damon Stoudamire, ending his tenure at the school after three seasons.

title: ⏱️ Last-minute NFL free agency nuggets
content: What are we hearing as NFL free agency nears? We give you the latest buzz on top players and teams that could be active.

title: Women's Bracketology: No. 1 seeds set, but bubble is toughest in years
content: Texas and South Carolina are locks for 1-seeds. TCU replaces Michigan as the final 2-seed. The bubble? Wide open.

title: NHL playoff watch: Who will win the Pacific Division title?
content: It's Golden Knights-Oilers at 9:30 p.m. on ESPN. Plus, full playoff projections, draft lottery standings and more.

title: Who aced the trade deadline? Six winners, six losers
content: The final week was a fun one, with some late shockers. Here's who feels great now -- and who might not.

title: What’s the best option at QB for Jets? Here’s the latest
content: New York has plenty of options to revamp its quarterbacks room through free agency, trading and the draft.

title: 'LOL THATS IT?': MMA fighters react to UFC White House card
content: The polarizing fight card generated online comments from stars such as Conor McGregor and Sean O'Malley.

title: The pride and power of Iowa State basketball's Audi Crooks
content: Audi Crooks hears the haters. But Iowa State's star inherited resilience from her mom and joy from her dad. Her high-scoring game is the ultimate counterpoint.

title: Predicting the USWNT's starting lineup for next year's World Cup
content: With the 2027 Women's World Cup closer than you think, we look at the USWNT's roster and forecast the 11 players Emma Hayes will count on for the tournament.

title: F1's new rules create 'Mario Kart' racing in Australia season opener
content: F1 preseason has been dominated by criticism of its new rules, and while Australia's season opener boasted lots of passing, the critiques didn't stop.

title: Solak: Why Crosby fits perfectly with the Ravens' D -- and their Super Bowl window
content: Baltimore had to give up two first-round picks to get him, but Crosby can dominate in the Ravens' defense.

title: Reid's updated NFL draft QB Hot Board: Strengths and weaknesses for the top 12 passers
content: Who are the best quarterbacks in the 2026 class? We sized up the top 12, starting with Fernando Mendoza and Ty Simpson.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Bernardo Silva set to leave Man City in summer
content: Bernardo Silva is expected to leave Man City, while Real Madrid have taken an interest in Newcastle's Sandro Tonali. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Steve Emt's incredible story: From UConn basketball to three-time Paralympian
content: Former UConn basketball player Steve Emt was paralyzed in a car accident at 25. Then he discovered his immense skill in Paralympic curling.

title: Khamenei's Son Mojtaba Named Iran's New Supreme Leader
content: Iran on Monday named Mojtaba Khamenei to succeed his father Ali Khamenei as Supreme Leader, signaling that hardliners remain firmly in charge in Tehran a week into its conflict with the United States and Israel.

title: US-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Mojtaba Appointed Supreme Leader By Decisive Vote, Says Iran Body
content: The war in the Middle East was triggered after the US and Israel attacked Iran last Saturday, killing Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

title: "Khamenei's Name To Continue": Iran Cleric's Big Hint On Next Supreme Leader
content: Iran on Sunday indicated it had chosen Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's son Mojtaba as his successor, after Israel struck fuel depots in Tehran overnight.

title: How Numbers Game Plays Out In Opposition's No-Trust Motion Against Speaker
content: The second phase of Parliament's Budget Session begins on Monday, and the first debate is expected to be on a no-confidence motion against the Speaker of the Lok Sabha, Om Birla.

title: Indian National Among 2 Killed After 'Projectile Strike' In Saudi Arabia
content: A projectile killed two people and injured 12 more in Saudi Arabia's Al Kharj governorate, south of Riyadh, the kingdom's civil defence said on Sunday, as Iran continued to launch missiles and drones at its Gulf neighbours.

title: "Betrayed By Congress": Assam Regional Party Slams Alliance Breakdown
content: Raijor Dal president and Sivasagar MLA Akhil Gogoi on Sunday said that the Congress has "betrayed" them by not informing them about an alliance meeting.

title: Vir Chakra Awardee, 1965 India-Pakistan War Hero KG George Dies At 95
content: Lance Havildar K G George (retd), who was awarded a Vir Chakra for his heroics in the 1965 India-Pakistan War, passed away in Kerala at the age of 95, his family said on Sunday.

title: "What's Not Worth It? Wife Or Marriage": TVK Worker To Party Leader Vijay
content: A worker of Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam or TVK has publicly criticised actor-turned-politician Vijay over the remarks he made while indirectly referring to the controversy surrounding his reported marital dispute.

title: Kheibar, Emad, Ghadr: Iran Launches Multiple Ballistic Missiles At Israel
content: Iran's elite Guards have launched ballistic missiles towards Israeli and US bases as the Middle East remains on the edge in the second week of war.

title: 2 Accused In Bangladesh Leader Osman Hadi's Murder Arrested By Bengal Police
content: Two persons accused of killing Osman Hadi were arrested by the special task force (STF) of the West Bengal police, an official said.

title: Tomcats Down? Israel Strikes Iran's Prized American-Made Fighter Jets
content: The Israeli Air Force said on Sunday that it carried out strikes on facilities housing Iran's F-14 Tomcat fighter jets at an airport in Isfahan.

title: First Came Inferno, Then Darkness: Tehran 'Blacked Out' After Oil Depots Hit
content: Residents of Tehran woke up on Sunday expecting daylight. Instead, they found the city still shrouded in darkness.

title: "Enemy's Misconceptions": Iran President's U-Turn After Apology To Neighbours
content: Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian appeared to backtrack from his conciliatory remark towards his Gulf neighbours.

title: Over 500 From Andhra Pradesh Stranded In Middle East Return To India
content: Over 500 people from Andhra Pradesh, who were stranded in West Asia due to the ongoing conflict between the US-Israel and Iran, have returned to the state, a non-resident Telugu society office bearer said.

title: Regime Change, Uranium Stockpile, And The US Dilemma To Invade Iran
content: The Iran war rages through its second week as Washington maintains suspense over invading the Gulf nation to achieve its end goals.

title: Man, 21, Arrested For Stabbing Lover Over Suspected Affair In Dehradun: Cops
content: A 21-year-old postgraduate student was arrested on Sunday for allegedly stabbing his girlfriend following a dispute over a suspected affair in the Clement Town area here, police said.

title: Dalit Man Dies In Police Custody In Tamil Nadu, Family Alleges Torture
content: A 26-year-old Dalit man died while undergoing treatment after being arrested by police in Tamil Nadu's Sivaganga district, with his family alleging that he was tortured in custody.

title: Man Arrested For Drowning 3 Minor Daughters In Lake In Telangana: Cops
content: An auto driver in the Kamareddy district of Telangana was arrested for allegedly killing his three minor daughters by drowning them in a lake, police said on Sunday.

title: "Why Governor Resigned," Asks Mamata Banerjee. "Confidential," Says CV Bose
content: Former West Bengal Governor CV Bose, whose sudden resignation has raised eyebrows, has now said the reason is "confidential" but he will share it at an "appropriate" time.

title: 3-Year-Old Gets AI-Based Cochlear Implant At Hospital In Rajasthan
content: In a first for the government hospitals in the state, an artificial intelligence-based smart cochlear implant has been implanted in the ear of a three-year-old girl at the Rajasthan University of Health Sciences here.

title: Scientists turn plastic into vinegar using sunlight at University of Waterloo
content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world. We are committed to demystifying the intricacies of science, making it accessible and engaging for readers of all backgrounds. title: Death behind bars in Chhattisgarh
content: The custodial death of a tribal leader lodged in a jail in Chhattisgarh’s Kanker district has sparked a debate over prisoner safety across the State. A report on what transpired in the Assembly and the family’s claims

title: Cafes, salons, community spaces bustle as Hyderabad celebrates Women’s Day
content: From small surprises at home to leisurely outings and self care breaks, residents found their own ways to honour the women in their lives and acknowledge the many roles they play in everyday life

title: One Nation, One Election — remedy worse than disease
content: India’s ‘One Nation, One Election’ proposal gravely undermines federalism, governance, democratic principles and the voter mandate

title: T.N. forms panel to frame rules to ban hostile dog breeds
content: The committee will recommend norms to regulate or ban aggressive dog breeds in the interest of public safety

title: Indian Women's Press Corps reiterates commitment to support safe, equitable spaces in media
content: It further said it was important that people listen to what women have to say in this complex, ever-changing world, adding that they must have a say in agendas that shape the policies, systems and indeed the world

title: Darjeeling district officials had raised alarm over lack of arrangements at venue
content: In the absence of the Chief Minister and Governor, the Minister-in-Waiting should have received the President according to protocol, officials said

title: On Women’s Day, cash incentives announced in Assam, Manipur
content: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma says the government will transfer ₹9,000 each to around 40 lakh beneficiary families under the Orunodoi scheme

title: Rules not ready for new rural jobs Act
content: The government has to come up with “objective parameters” for normative allocation of budget to States, and formulate “development parameters” to categorise Gram Panchayats into three types as mandated

title: Government launches logo design contest for VB-G RAM G Act
content: The Ministry said it seeks to promote public participation in branding the programme, which is a key pillar of the government’s rural development strategy aligned with the vision of “Viksit Bharat 2047”

title: Jaishankar to speak on West Asia conflict in Parliament on March 9
content: Opposition has criticised government for its “silence” on Khamenei’s killing, torpedoing of Iranian ship by a U.S. submarine in the Indian ocean; India has “strongly condemned” the attacks on Gulf countries by Iran

title: Mamata should resign over protocol breach at President Murmu’s Darjeeling event: BJP
content: PM Modi had insulted the President by remaining seated with veteran BJP leader L.K. Advani while President Murmu stood next to them, says the West Bengal CM, holding a dharna against the SIR in Kolkata

title: Gulf crisis: Air India adds flights to Europe & US to meet demand

title: ISRO and ESA sign agreement for Earth Observation missions
content: The agreement was signed on March 4 by M. Ganesh Pillai, scientific secretary, ISRO, and Simonetta Cheli, director, Earth Observation Programme, ESA, in a virtual meeting mode

title: Indian Railways launch ‘SHINE’ to empower women at workplace
content: Operational on International Women’s Day, the app enables women to lodge complaints of sexual harassment

title: NCB busts pan-India darknet drug network called ‘Team Kalki’
content: The two accused, who have been arrested, are ‘habitual offenders and were previously arrested in cases registered under the Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances Act”, the agency says

title: Children lead No-Plastic run on Krishna river bank
content: Over 500 participants join awareness run organised by AWARA and Aravinda Schools to highlight dangers of plastic pollution in Vijayawada

title: Uttam pledges to make Palamuru-Rangareddy fully operational in 30 months
content: Irrigation Minister vows to complete the Kalwakurthy, Nettempadu, Bhima and Koilsagar projects by March 2027 while reviewing the status of works on the projects; states that land acquisition has been given top priority

title: Rumours of export disruption trigger panic selling by aqua farmers in Andhra Pradesh
content: Farmers rush to harvest shrimp and sell at lower prices amid fears of shipping delays; officials say Israel-Iran conflict has little impact on marine exports from the State

title: India taps alternative crude supplies as Iran conflict drags on
content: Indian refiners have begun negotiating for additional crude cargoes from the U.S., Russia and West Africa to ensure supplies remain adequate; refineries, which convert crude oil into fuels like petrol and diesel, have deferred planned maintenance shutdowns and are maintaining normal processing rates to create buffers

title: From tariff turbulence to trade resilience, Andhra Pradesh anchors India’s shrimp export growth
content: After a 15% dip in U.S. shipments, Andhra Pradesh shifted 57% of exports to alternative markets, posting over US$2.5 billion in seafood exports in 2024-25

title: Tens of thousands of people attend 204th Sharanabasaveshwar Rathotsava
content: The historic rathotsava commemorates the death anniversary of 18th century saint and social reformer Sharanabasaveshwar and it continues to attract devotees cutting across caste, creed and communal lines

title: Kozhikode City police launches ‘Nirbhaya Nisha’
content: The initiative seeks to provide prompt police assistance and strengthen security arrangements for women commuting during later hours

title: Quantum leap for tribal girl students in NPTEL exam
content: 57 students, mostly girls from a remote Government Degree College in Chintapalli, appear for the quantum computing examination in Visakhapatnam after the Principal pays the ₹500 fee for those who could not afford it on International Women’s Day

title: Karnataka notifies reservation for 369 wards of new Bengaluru corporations, questions persist over quotas
content: However, the final notification is likely to be challenged in the court, as the Urban Development Department has not changed the percentage in either vertical reservation or the women quota, despite the draft notification drawing widespread criticism over the same.

title: Karnataka High Court judge injured in convoy accident on Bengaluru–Tumakuru Highway
content: According to the police, the incident occurred at around 7.30 a.m. when a truck travelling ahead of the judge’s convoy suddenly took a left turn.

title: ‘Each One Teach One’ model launched in Mandya to improve SSLC results
content: ZP CEO Nandini K.R. said while about 68% students had passed in Preparatory Examination-1, the pass percentage increased to 86 per cent in Preparatory Examination-3. Officials asked to further improve the results to reach 95 per cent

title: BJP slams Pinarayi for supporting Iran, calls it ‘appeasement politics’
content: BJP State president Rajeev Chandrasekhar asks CM why he is silent on attacks by Iran on Gulf Cooperation Council countries, where large number of Keralites reside

title: Sobha Surendran launches campaign in Palakkad; district BJP leader’s absence fuels speculation
content: Absence of BJP East district president Prasanth Sivan from the leader’s programmes sparks speculation about growing dissent within the party and his dissatisfaction over being denied the Palakkad seat

title: Mini-tech park project to get under way on March 9 at Cyberpark in Kozhikode
content: The tech park aims to provide modern office facilities for technology companies, global enterprises, start-ups, and global capability centres

title: Visakhapatnam metro rail project to enhance traffic flow with new flyovers
content: NHAI proposed to construct 12 flyovers along NH-16 passing through Visakhapatnam to ensure smooth flow of freight and passenger traffic, says A.P. Metro Rail Corporation MD

title: A Class 10 student punches lab assistant at Veeravalli ZPHS in Krishna district, video goes viral
content: The DEO conducts inquiry, student counselled while action proposed against librarian who filmed and released clip on social media

title: Kerala plantation workers’ daily pay increased by ₹48
content: Labourers in the rubber, tea, coffee, and cardamom sectors covered. As interim relief, workers will receive a one-time payment of ₹1,000

title: Nitish Kumar’s Rajya Sabha move may lead to ‘ideological takeover’ in Bihar politics: CPI(ML) Liberation
content: CPI(ML) Liberation general secretary Dipankar Bhattacharya claims development reflects the growing dominance of the BJP over its ally Janata Dal (United)

title: LDF seeks to punch holes in Rahul Gandhi’s election promises
content: Transport Minister K.B. Ganesh Kumar says Rahul’s election promise of free travel for women on KSRTC will ruin the financially struggling corporation

title: 18 MedTech innovations under pilot testing in A.P. government hospitals
content: Technologies ranging from AI-powered diagnostics to tele-medicine and non-invasive tools being tested in hospitals across the State as part of MedTech Innovation Challenge launched by the government

title: Pawan Kalyan, Lokesh salute women on International Women’s Day
content: True progress of a nation is measured by the dignity, safety and empowerment of its women, says the Deputy Chief Minister, while HRD Minister says that respecting women is a moral value that every one should demonstrate in practice

title: Row over Thrikkarippur seat intensifies
content: The protest grew stronger after indications emerged that the Kerala Congress was being given priority in the seat-sharing talks despite having only a limited presence in the constituency

title: India earned great respect by remaining neutral between global powers, says Kharge
content: The Leader of Opposition in the Rajya Sabha says PM Modi is harming India’s global standing through his foreign policy decisions

title: Kerala’s diaspora engagement in higher education draws interest from Sri Lanka
content: In a virtual meeting, Sri Lankan officials discuss Scholar Connect initiative with KSHEC representatives, explore possibilities of integrating it with ‘Praja Shakthi’, a flagship development programme launched by the Anura Kumara Dissanayake-led administration.

title: Karnataka Budget 2026-27: Share of Central funds decreasing by the year adds to fiscal pressure
content: Medium Term Fiscal Plan shows the share of Central transfers in the State’s resource envelope reduced from 32.1% in 2017-18 to 25.1% in 2026-27, a drop of 21.8%

title: Government launches campaign against ‘Sarpanch Pati’ practice
content: ministry of panchayati raj launched social media campaign to spotlight 'sarpanch Pati' practice . campaign aims to raise awareness against proxy representation where elected women Sarpanches are sidelined while male relatives exercise authority in their name .

title: J&K man booked for posts on protests over West Asia war
content: police say the content was provocative and intended at disturbing law and order in Kashmir . this is the first time after recent widespread protests in Kashmir that the police have officially acknowledged lodging an FIR in the matter . a man running the Instagram page “Master Electrician” has uploaded videos related to a recent protest over the ongoing situation in west Asia .

title: Congress will ‘regret’ move against Om Birla: Rijiju on Parliament session eve
content: the main opposition party would end up regretting its move against the Chair, he said . an extensive discussion will begin from Mo nda y, and members from BJP-led NDA are likely to frame the notice submitted by 118 MPs .

title: Only 10% of MPs/MLAs women, says ADR report
content: 74 (14%) are women among the 543 MPs in the 18th Lok Sabha . as many as 22 states and UTs have women MPs . only large state without a woman MP is Kerala .

title: ‘Arrogance will be shattered’: PM Modi slams TMC on President row
content: prime minister stepped up his attack on the govt over alleged 'mismanagement' of an event attended by president Droupadi Murmu . he accuses the BJP of insulting the head of state and warns that the party's "arrogance of power will soon be shattered"

title: Why Nepal's vote count crawls: Each ballot paper lifted, shown to reps, okayed before next picked
content: the pace is set by a single sheet of paper . a ballot is lifted from the pile, unfolded and held up . every voter casts two separate ballots for the 165 fifi votes . in post-Sept 2025, the uprising left 77 people dead .

title: Sailor from sunken Iranian warship called father before US torpedo strike: Report
content: a sailor of the Iranian warship IRIS Dena called his father over phone minutes before the frigate was struck by a torpedo . despite the twin US warnings, the commander refused to allow the crew to abandon the ship, the report said . the 32 sailors survived the attack were basically those who managed to escape on lifeboats .

title: B.C. fires hoops coach Grant after five years
content: Boston College has fired men's basketball coach Earl Grant . he has three seasons remaining on his contract . the school has "greater expectations" for the basketball program . "we will begin a national search immediately for a new head coach," athletic director says .
